_comment: Record of the last independent cross-check of deploy/sandbox.Dockerfile's pinned pandoc / Chrome-for-Testing sha256 digests. Regenerate by bumping PANDOC_VERSION/CHROME_FOR_TESTING_VERSION in the Dockerfile, then re-running scripts/verify-pinned-checksums.sh and pasting its output into this file. tests/test_pinned_checksum_verification.py asserts this file's versions and hashes stay in lockstep with deploy/sandbox.Dockerfile, so a version bump without an updated record fails CI instead of silently going unverified. See SECURITY.md ('Known follow-ups') for the full writeup.

pandoc.method: GitHub Releases API per-asset `digest` field (sha256, computed server-side by GitHub at upload time -- independent of the maintainer who recorded the Dockerfile pin), cross-checked against a fresh independent download of the same release asset.

chrome_for_testing.method: Chrome for Testing does not publish a sha256 anywhere in its own manifests -- confirmed by fetching both googlechromelabs.github.io/chrome-for-testing/known-good-versions-with-downloads.json and last-known-good-versions-with-downloads.json and finding zero 'sha256'/hash fields in either, for any version, including the current Stable release. As a substitute independent cross-check, Google Cloud Storage's own backend computes and serves an `x-goog-hash: md5=...` header for every object in the chrome-for-testing-public bucket at upload time, independent of whoever later self-computed a sha256 for the Dockerfile pin. This record cross-checks: (1) the Dockerfile-pinned sha256 against a fresh independent re-download's sha256, and (2) that same fresh download's md5 against GCS's own server-reported md5 for the object. Note the pinned CHROME_FOR_TESTING_VERSION (148.0.7778.179) is also NOT present in the 'known-good-versions' list at all (only 148.0.7778.178 is) -- it was never part of the automated smoke-test-and-promote pipeline, though it is a real, directly downloadable build under the same public GCS path.
